title: Technical Design
oldText: The backend exposes document routes through Flask.
newText: The backend exposes document routes through Flask. Authentication uses email and password login, with signed tokens protecting private routes.
referenceSummary: Added email-and-password authentication and signed-token protection for private backend routes.

title: Milestone Scope
oldText: Users can create documents, save versions, compare changes, and export documents as PDF.
newText: Users can create documents, save versions, and compare changes.
referenceSummary: Removed PDF export from the milestone scope.

title: Collaboration Design
oldText: Collaborators can open a shared document and submit their edits to the server.
newText: Collaborators can edit the same shared document simultaneously, while Operational Transformation resolves concurrent operations before the server broadcasts them.
referenceSummary: Clarified that collaborators edit simultaneously and that Operational Transformation resolves concurrent changes before broadcast.

title: Problem Motivation
oldText: Real-time editors can make revision history difficult to understand.
newText: Real-time editors can make document revision history harder to follow.
referenceSummary: Reworded the problem statement without changing its meaning.

title: User Guide
oldText: Select two saved versions to compare them. The comparison highlights inserted and deleted text. Select two saved versions to compare them.
newText: Select two saved versions to compare them. The comparison highlights inserted and deleted text.
referenceSummary: Removed a repeated instruction about selecting versions for comparison.

title: Version Control Flow
oldText: A user can save a named version. Versions appear in chronological order. The editor always shows the latest saved version.
newText: A user can save a named version with an optional note. Versions appear newest first. Restoring a version replaces the working document content without changing its title.
referenceSummary: Expanded version management with optional notes, newest-first ordering, and content-only restoration.

title: Database Migration Plan
oldText: The proof of concept uses SQLite. The production option is undecided.
newText: The proof of concept uses SQLite. Production metadata will use PostgreSQL, while document files may be stored in S3.
referenceSummary: Specified PostgreSQL for production metadata and S3 as a possible document-storage service.
forbiddenTerms: replaced SQLite in the proof of concept

title: Project Introduction
oldText: NotGoogleDocs is a collaborative editor it provides explicit version snapshots.
newText: NotGoogleDocs is a collaborative editor; it provides explicit version snapshots.
referenceSummary: Corrected punctuation without changing the document's meaning.
